valencian-rice-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Valencian Rice Recipe

ingredients.item: 400 g  carne de porco em cubos
1 small can of tomato in pieces (390 g)

instructions: Take a large pot of butter and oil to the light. Add the pork and let it saute. Add the tomato and let it cook for 10 minutes until it becomes soft.

Then add the carrots, peeled and cut into slices, and the pepper, clean and cut into strips. Cover the pot and leave the kitchen for another 5 minutes. Then add the squid rings and clams and let cook for 5 minutes.

Cook the gambas in salted water for 5 minutes. Drain and reserve the broth after strain. Add the rice and peas to the squid pot and meat and stir. Measure the broth of the gambas and add more water to get a little more than twice the volume of the rice (about 850 ml).

Dilute in this liquid a small portion of saffron, add to the rice, boil, season with salt and pepper and cook for 10 minutes. Just before you finish cooking, add the sausages cut into slices and the cooked gambas. Involve and serve at once.

vegan-chocolate-chip-cookies.name: Vegan Chocolate Chip Cookies

ingredients.item: . vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract
. diamond crystal or 1 0.25 tsp. morton kosher salt
. vegan semisweet chocolate (such as scharffen berger)

instructions: Place racks in upper and lower thirds of oven; preheat to 350°. Whisk 1 cup (packed; 200 g) organic dark brown sugar, ⅓ cup (67 g) organic granulated sugar, ⅓ cup Coca-Cola or water, ⅓ cup extra-virgin olive oil, ⅓ cup neutral oil (such as avocado or grapeseed), and 1 Tbsp. vanilla bean paste or vanilla extract in a large bowl until sugar dissolves slightly, 30–60 seconds. Add 2¾ cups (344 g) all-purpose flour, 2 tsp. Diamond Crystal or 1¼ tsp. Morton Kosher salt, 1 tsp. baking powder, and 1 tsp. baking soda and stir with a rubber spatula until no pockets of dry flour remain. Add 6 oz. vegan semisweet chocolate, coarsely chopped, and stir to evenly distribute throughout dough.

Using a #40 cookie scoop or 2 (heaping) Tbsp., portion out dough and roll into smooth balls. (The chocolate chunks have a tendency to separate from the dough, so try your best to keep them tucked in. Washing your hands halfway through rolling the balls will help prevent sticking.) Place balls on 3 parchment-lined baking sheets (or 2 if that is all you’ve got), spacing about 2" apart (you should have about 9 cookies per sheet). Bake 2 sheets of cookies, rotating baking sheets top to bottom and front to back halfway through, until tops of cookies are puffy and cracked, 10–12 minutes. (Cookies will be very soft and appear almost undercooked. Do not be tempted to overbake.) Remove from oven and firmly tap baking sheets on counter once or twice to deflate cookies. Let cookies cool on baking sheets. Repeat with remaining baking sheet of cookies (or use a cool baking sheet lined with a fresh sheet of parchment if needed), baking on either rack.
Do Ahead: Dough can be made 3 days ahead; tightly cover and chill. Bake directly from fridge. Cookies can be baked 5 days ahead; store airtight at room temperature.
Editor’s note: Head this way for more vegan cookie recipes and other dairy-free desserts →

vegan-roasted-red-pepper-and-carrot-soup.name: Vegan Roasted Red Pepper And Carrot Soup

ingredients.prep: peeled and cut into 1/2 inch pieces

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C).
Mix carrots, red bell peppers, onion, garlic, paprika, cumin, salt, and pepper together in a bowl. Transfer to a rimmed baking sheet. Drizzle with olive oil and toss to coat.
Roast in the preheated oven until tender, about 30 minutes.

Bring vegetable broth, bay leaf, and oregano to a boil in a soup pot. Add roasted vegetables to the pot. Remove bay leaf and add red pepper flakes. Simmer for 15 minutes, then blend with an immersion blender.
Add apple cider vinegar to puréed soup. Serve with hot sauce.

vegan-waffles-step-by-step-comedera.name: Vegan Waffles, Step By Step
instructions: Add all the ingredients and select them for 1 minute.
Waffle and spray some oil.
Echa la masa en la waflera con la ayuda de una cuchara y cocina durante unos 7 minutos.
Sirve con tus toppings preferidos. Frutas, chocolate, o lo que prefieras.

vegetable-crudites-what-they-are-and-how.name: Vegetable Crudités, What They Are And How To Prepare This Healthy Appetizer
vegetable-crudites-what-they-are-and-how.original_name: Crudités de verduras, qué son y cómo preparar este saludable aperitivo
instructions: First of all, we have to clear up every vegetable. That looks obvious, but when they're gonna eat raw, it's important to give them another moment. Carrots (3 units), celery (3 sizes), cucumbers (1 large) and yellow pepper (1 unit) should be rinsed under the tap and, if you can, leave them 10 minutes with water with a vinegar or lemon chorrit to remove potential ground remains or bacteria. Then just dry them with paper or a clean cloth.
Peel carrots and carrots into rods about 8 or 10 cm.
Haz lo mismo con el pepino.
The celery, for its part, cuts into strips of the same length, withdrawing the toughest strands if they had them.
El pimiento amarillo queda muy vistoso si lo abres, le quitas las semillas y lo cortas en tiras anchas y regulares.

Finally, cherry tomatoes (about 12) require nothing but good washing. You can leave them whole or cut them in half as you propose them. I prefer to leave them whole, because they better preserve their juice and bring a round touch with them.

Once you have everything ready, place the vegetables onto a plate or a big fountain. It's okay to alternate colors: one group of carrot, another one of celery, then cucumbers, pepper and tomatoes at the end. At the centre, leave a hole for the hummus (200 g) or dip you will use. Keep the fountain in the refrigerator till they serve to keep the vegetables fresh and crispy.

vegetarian-black-bean-and-sweet-potato-t.name: Vegetarian Black Bean And Sweet Potato Tacos
instructions: Toss the sweet potatoes with the olive oil, salt, and pepper in a medium microwave-safe bowl, and microwave until tender, about 2 minutes.
Heat the tortillas in a dry skillet over low heat and fill with cooked sweet potatoes, black the beans, pico de gallo, and avocado. Garnish with cilantro.
Enjoy!

village-muffins-fluffy-and-plump-pequere.name: Village Muffins (fluffy And Plump)
village-muffins-fluffy-and-plump-pequere.original_name: Magdalenas caseras de pueblo (esponjosas y con copete)

ingredients.item: 100 ml de aceite de oliva virgen extra
2 (double) gasification sachets or 1 sachet of chemical yeast

instructions: We purify the oven at 200ºC We batiamos very well for a few minutes the eggs with sugar till the eggs whitens and the sugar dissolve. We added the lemon and milk and oil and we mixed them up. Finally we put the flour mixed with the gasification. I like to let this mix stand for half an hour.

Let's put the mixture into paper mold for muffins (we recommend those of this type), leave a free finger We scrubbed the surface with sugar We put into the pressurized oven, we lower the temperature to 180 ° C and baked about 15 minutes. We get out and we get cold and ready to sweetens our day!
